skill.name: changelog-maintenance
skill.description: Maintain clear changelogs, release notes, and migration guides using Keep a Changelog and semantic versioning conventions.
skill.summary: Create structured changelogs and release notes for software projects.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backticks, URLs, fetch examples, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are documentation examples inside SKILL.md. No executable scripts, command execution instructions, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Formatting Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to markdown code fences, inline API paths, version examples, and output format examples. No shell command or Ruby backtick execution is present in the skill instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible as markdown examples and inline documentation. I found no executable file or instruction that asks the agent to run these snippets.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Links and Local API Examples
low_findings.description: The network findings are standards links, placeholder GitHub comparison links, an example image URL, and fetch calls to local API paths inside a migration guide. They do not send data to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs reference public documentation or placeholders, and the fetch examples use relative paths. This supports a documentation-only interpretation rather than network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Legacy Authentication Example Is Contextual
low_findings.description: The Basic Auth and base64 example is labeled as the old v1.x behavior in a migration guide and is replaced with JWT usage. It is not an instruction to implement weak authentication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text explicitly frames Basic Auth as the previous behavior and later deprecation timeline. The example is used to explain migration, not to promote insecure implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Substantiated
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak cryptography detections do not correspond to cryptographic algorithm usage at the flagged lines. The lines contain the skill description or ordinary changelog prose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged lines found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic algorithm usage. The static hits appear to be pattern-matching artifacts.

content.value_statement: Release changes often become hard to track across features, fixes, and breaking changes. This skill helps create structured changelogs, release notes, and migration guides for clear software communication.
actual_capabilities: Structures CHANGELOG.md entries using Keep a Changelog sections.
Explains semantic version bumps for major, minor, and patch releases.
Drafts user-facing release notes from technical change lists.
Documents breaking changes with migration guidance and timelines.
Organizes release documentation files for changelogs, releases, and migrations.
limitations: Does not inspect git history unless the user provides repository context.
Does not verify whether listed changes are complete or shipped.
Does not replace legal, compliance, or security release review.
Uses example dates and versions unless project-specific values are supplied.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Product Release
use_cases.description: Turn feature, fix, and security notes into a clear release announcement for customers.

use_cases.title: Maintain Developer Changelogs
use_cases.description: Organize technical changes into Added, Changed, Deprecated, Removed, Fixed, and Security sections.

use_cases.title: Document Breaking Changes
use_cases.description: Create migration guidance that explains removed behavior, replacement APIs, and upgrade timing.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Changelog Entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a changelog entry for version [version] released on [date]. Changes: [paste feature, fix, and security notes].

prompt_templates.title: Convert Notes to Release Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these internal release notes into user-facing release notes. Keep technical details accurate and explain user impact clearly: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Semantic Version Bump
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changes and recommend the semantic version bump. Explain whether this is major, minor, or patch: [paste changes].

prompt_templates.title: Write a Migration Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration guide for these breaking changes. Include old behavior, new behavior, upgrade steps, deprecation timeline, and release note wording: [paste details].

output_examples.input: A release includes dark mode, faster search, a password reset fix, and updated dependencies.
output: A changelog section grouped under Added, Changed, Fixed, and Security.
A customer-friendly release note that explains visible improvements.
A suggested patch or minor version decision based on compatibility.

output_examples.input: An API removes Basic Authentication and changes the user list response.
output: A breaking changes section that names removed behavior and replacement behavior.
A migration guide outline with upgrade steps and timing.
Release wording that warns API consumers before adoption.

best_practices: Provide exact versions, release dates, and affected components before drafting.
Separate developer-facing changelog details from user-facing release notes.
Call out breaking changes, security fixes, and migration steps clearly.
anti_patterns: Do not paste raw git logs without explaining user impact.
Do not group every change under vague labels like miscellaneous updates.
Do not hide breaking changes inside general improvement sections.

faq.question: Can this skill generate a full CHANGELOG.md?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the version, date, and change list, and it can format a complete changelog entry.

faq.question: Does it follow Keep a Changelog?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses common sections such as Added, Changed, Deprecated, Removed, Fixed, and Security.

faq.question: Can it choose the next semantic version?
faq.answer: It can recommend major, minor, or patch based on the changes you provide.

faq.question: Can it write customer-facing release notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can rewrite technical notes into clear release notes focused on user impact.

faq.question: Can it create migration guides?
faq.answer: Yes. It can document old behavior, new behavior, upgrade steps, and deprecation timelines.

faq.question: Does it verify shipped changes automatically?
faq.answer: No. You must provide accurate project context, release scope, and shipped change details.
